# Skyvern

> Skyvern automates browser-based workflows using LLMs and computer vision. It navigates websites it has never seen before - filling forms, extracting data, and completing multi-step tasks - via a simple API. Use the Python SDK, TypeScript SDK, REST API, MCP server, or Cloud UI to build and run browser automations. Key capabilities: natural-language task execution, multi-step workflows with loops and conditionals, credential management (passwords, credit cards, TOTP/2FA), browser session persistence, data extraction, file downloads, webhook notifications, and proxy/geolocation support.

## Getting Started

- [What is Skyvern?](https://skyvern.com/docs/getting-started/introduction): Overview of Skyvern's AI-powered browser automation platform - how it uses LLMs and computer vision to navigate any website without custom scripts
- [Core Concepts](https://skyvern.com/docs/getting-started/core-concepts): Key building blocks - tasks, workflows, blocks, parameters, browser sessions, browser profiles, credentials, runs, and artifacts
- [API Quickstart](https://skyvern.com/docs/getting-started/quickstart): Run your first browser automation task with the Skyvern REST API using Python, TypeScript, or cURL in under 5 minutes
- [AI Agents Quickstart](https://skyvern.com/docs/getting-started/ai-agents-quickstart): Set up Skyvern's MCP server so Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, or any AI coding assistant can run browser automations

## Creating & Running Tasks

- [Run a Task](https://skyvern.com/docs/running-automations/run-a-task): Execute a single browser automation with a natural-language goal - configure URLs, navigation goals, data extraction schemas, webhooks, and proxy settings via API or SDK
- [Task Parameters](https://skyvern.com/docs/running-automations/task-parameters): Configure task behavior - proxy location, browser session reuse, TOTP/2FA credentials, max steps, webhook callbacks, error handling, and extraction schemas
- [Extract Structured Data](https://skyvern.com/docs/running-automations/extract-structured-data): Define JSON schemas to get consistent, typed data extraction output from tasks - prices, addresses, table rows, and more

## Building Multi-Step Workflows

- [Build a Workflow](https://skyvern.com/docs/multi-step-automations/build-a-workflow): Create multi-step browser automations by chaining task blocks, loops, conditionals, extraction, and file operations into reusable workflows
- [Workflow Blocks Reference](https://skyvern.com/docs/multi-step-automations/workflow-blocks-reference): Complete reference for every block type - navigation block, action block, extract block, loop block, text prompt block, login block, file operations, email, webhook, and validation blocks
- [Workflow Parameters](https://skyvern.com/docs/multi-step-automations/workflow-parameters): Pass dynamic values into workflows at runtime - configure proxy, webhook, browser session, and credential parameters per run
- [File Operations](https://skyvern.com/docs/multi-step-automations/file-operations): Download, parse (CSV, PDF, Excel), and upload files within workflow blocks
- [Scheduling Workflows](https://skyvern.com/docs/multi-step-automations/scheduling-workflows): Run workflows automatically on a recurring cron schedule via API, SDK, or the Cloud UI

## Handling Authentication

- [Store Credentials](https://skyvern.com/docs/credentials/store-credentials): Securely manage login passwords, credit cards, TOTP secrets, and API keys - with Bitwarden, 1Password, Azure Key Vault, or Skyvern's built-in vault
- [Handle 2FA](https://skyvern.com/docs/credentials/handle-2fa): Configure TOTP-based two-factor authentication so Skyvern can complete login flows that require 2FA codes
- [Troubleshooting Login](https://skyvern.com/docs/credentials/troubleshooting-login): Debug common authentication failures - credential issues, CAPTCHA blocks, bot detection, and session expiration

## Optimizing Performance

- [Browser Sessions](https://skyvern.com/docs/optimization/browser-sessions): Persist a live browser across multiple tasks and workflows - maintain login state, cookies, and page context between API calls
- [Browser Profiles](https://skyvern.com/docs/optimization/browser-profiles): Save and reuse authenticated browser state (cookies, localStorage) across runs without re-logging in
- [Cost Control](https://skyvern.com/docs/optimization/cost-control): Limit max steps, optimize prompts, and use caching to manage credit consumption

## Shipping to Production

- [Webhooks](https://skyvern.com/docs/going-to-production/webhooks): Receive HTTP callbacks when tasks and workflow runs complete, fail, or require human intervention
- [Proxy & Geolocation](https://skyvern.com/docs/going-to-production/proxy-geolocation): Route browser traffic through residential proxies in specific countries and regions to handle geo-restricted content
- [Error Handling](https://skyvern.com/docs/going-to-production/error-handling): Detect, classify, and recover from task failures - retry strategies, cancel runs, and webhook error payloads
- [Reliability Tips](https://skyvern.com/docs/going-to-production/reliability-tips): Write robust prompts, handle edge cases, and build resilient automations that work across website changes
- [CAPTCHA & Bot Detection](https://skyvern.com/docs/going-to-production/captcha-bot-detection): How Skyvern handles CAPTCHAs, anti-bot systems, and stealth browser configuration

## Connecting Tools & Platforms

- [MCP Server](https://skyvern.com/docs/going-to-production/mcp): Install and configure Skyvern's Model Context Protocol server for Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, and other AI assistants
- [CLI & Skills](https://skyvern.com/docs/going-to-production/cli): Run tasks, manage workflows, store credentials, and open browser sessions from the command line
- [Zapier](https://skyvern.com/docs/integrations/zapier): Trigger browser automations from Zapier workflows - run tasks and get results via Zapier actions
- [Make.com](https://skyvern.com/docs/integrations/make): Integrate Skyvern browser automations into Make.com scenarios
- [n8n](https://skyvern.com/docs/integrations/n8n): Run browser automations as nodes in n8n workflows
- [Workato](https://skyvern.com/docs/integrations/workato): Run browser automations in Workato recipes with the Skyvern connector
- [Local LLMs](https://skyvern.com/docs/integrations/local-llms): Run Skyvern with Ollama, LiteLLM, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint for self-hosted LLM inference

## SDK Reference

The Skyvern SDK is available for Python (`skyvern`) and TypeScript (`@skyvern/client`). All reference pages show both languages side-by-side.

**For a single-page view of every SDK method, parameter, and type, see [Complete Reference](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/complete-reference).**

### Overview & Setup

- [SDK Overview](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/overview): Install, authenticate, configure - constructor parameters, environments, local mode, wait_for_completion, request options
- [Error Handling](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/error-handling): Error types (BadRequestError, NotFoundError, etc.), catching errors, timeouts, retries, abort signals, run failure vs API errors
- [Complete Reference](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/complete-reference): Every method, parameter, and type on one page

### Tasks

- [run_task](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/tasks/run-task): Start a browser automation with a natural-language prompt - 23 parameters including engine, max_steps, data_extraction_schema, proxy, webhooks
- [get_run](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/tasks/get-run): Get current status and results of any run (task or workflow)
- [cancel_run](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/tasks/cancel-run): Cancel a running or queued run
- [get_run_timeline](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/tasks/get-run-timeline): Get step-by-step timeline of a run with screenshots and reasoning
- [get_run_artifacts](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/tasks/get-run-artifacts): Get all artifacts (screenshots, recordings, generated code) for a run
- [get_artifact](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/tasks/get-artifact): Get a single artifact by ID
- [get_runs_v2](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/tasks/get-runs-v2): List all runs with filtering and pagination
- [retry_run_webhook](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/tasks/retry-run-webhook): Re-send webhook notification for a completed run

### Workflows

- [run_workflow](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/workflows/run-workflow): Execute a workflow by permanent ID with parameters, browser profiles, and proxy configuration
- [create_workflow](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/workflows/create-workflow): Create a new workflow from JSON or YAML definition
- [get_workflow](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/workflows/get-workflow): Get a specific workflow by permanent ID
- [get_workflows](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/workflows/get-workflows): List all workflows with filtering and pagination
- [get_workflow_versions](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/workflows/get-workflow-versions): List all versions of a workflow
- [update_workflow](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/workflows/update-workflow): Update an existing workflow definition (creates a new version)
- [delete_workflow](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/workflows/delete-workflow): Delete a workflow
- [get_workflow_runs](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/workflows/get-workflow-runs): List workflow runs across all workflows with status and search filters
- [update_workflow_folder](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/workflows/update-workflow-folder): Move a workflow to a different folder

### Browser Sessions

- [create_browser_session](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-sessions/create-browser-session): Create a persistent cloud browser session with timeout, proxy, and extension options
- [get_browser_session](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-sessions/get-browser-session): Get session status and details
- [get_browser_sessions](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-sessions/get-browser-sessions): List all active browser sessions
- [close_browser_session](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-sessions/close-browser-session): Close a browser session and release resources

### Browser Profiles

- [create_browser_profile](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-profiles/create-browser-profile): Save browser state (cookies, localStorage) from a completed run as a reusable profile
- [list_browser_profiles](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-profiles/list-browser-profiles): List all browser profiles
- [get_browser_profile](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-profiles/get-browser-profile): Get a single profile by ID
- [delete_browser_profile](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-profiles/delete-browser-profile): Delete a browser profile

### Credentials

- [create_credential](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/credentials/create-credential): Store a new credential (password, credit card, or secret)
- [get_credential](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/credentials/get-credential): Get credential metadata by ID
- [get_credentials](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/credentials/get-credentials): List all credentials (values never returned)
- [update_credential](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/credentials/update-credential): Overwrite stored credential data while keeping the same ID
- [delete_credential](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/credentials/delete-credential): Delete a credential
- [send_totp_code](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/credentials/send-totp-code): Send a TOTP code to Skyvern during a 2FA-requiring run

### Helpers

- [login](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/helpers/login): Automate login using stored credentials - supports Skyvern vault, Bitwarden, 1Password, Azure Key Vault
- [download_files](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/helpers/download-files): Navigate to a page and download files with AI
- [upload_file](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/helpers/upload-file): Upload a file to Skyvern storage, get a presigned URL

### Browser Automation - SkyvernBrowser

Launch and manage browser connections. Both Python and TypeScript SDKs support cloud browsers. Python additionally supports local browser launch.

- [launch_cloud_browser](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/launch-cloud-browser): Create a new cloud browser session and connect to it
- [use_cloud_browser](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/use-cloud-browser): Reuse the most recent cloud session or create a new one
- [connect_to_cloud_browser_session](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/connect-to-cloud-browser-session): Connect to an existing cloud browser session by ID
- [connect_to_browser_over_cdp](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/connect-to-browser-over-cdp): Connect to any browser via Chrome DevTools Protocol
- [launch_local_browser](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/launch-local-browser): Launch a local Chromium browser (Python only, requires Skyvern.local())
- [get_working_page](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/get-working-page): Get the most recent page or create one
- [new_page](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/new-page): Create a new page (tab)
- [get_page_for](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/get-page-for): Wrap an existing Playwright Page with Skyvern AI (Python only)
- [close](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/close): Close the browser and release resources

### Browser Automation - SkyvernPage

AI-enhanced Playwright methods on a live browser page. Pass a CSS selector for standard Playwright behavior, add a prompt for AI fallback, or use prompt alone for pure AI.

- [act](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/act): Perform a freeform AI action described in natural language
- [extract](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/extract): Extract structured data from the page using AI with optional JSON schema
- [validate](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/validate): Assert a condition about the page state - returns bool
- [prompt](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/prompt): Send a question to the LLM about the current page with optional schema
- [locator](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/locator): Locate an element using selector, AI prompt, or both - returns a chainable Locator/AILocator (Python only for AI mode)
- [click](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/click): Click an element - selector, AI prompt, or both with fallback
- [fill](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/fill): Fill an input field - selector, AI prompt, or both with fallback
- [select_option](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/select-option): Select a dropdown option - selector, AI prompt, or both with fallback
- [type](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/type): Type text character-by-character - triggers keystroke events (Python only)
- [hover](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/hover): Move mouse over an element (Python only)
- [scroll](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/scroll): Scroll page by pixel offset - scroll_x and scroll_y (Python only)
- [upload_file](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/upload-file): Upload files to a file input (Python only)
- [fill_form](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/fill-form): AI-powered single-page form fill from a data dict (Python only)
- [fill_multipage_form](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/fill-multipage-form): AI form fill across multiple pages with automatic navigation (Python only)
- [fill_from_mapping](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/fill-from-mapping): Fill form fields using an explicit index-to-value mapping (Python only)
- [extract_form_fields](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/extract-form-fields): Extract all form fields with metadata from the page (Python only)
- [validate_mapping](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/validate-mapping): Validate that a field mapping will work on the current form (Python only)
- [fill_autocomplete](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/fill-autocomplete): Fill an input with typeahead/autocomplete handling (Python only)
- [frame_switch](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/frame-switch): Switch working context to an iframe (Python only)
- [frame_main](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/frame-main): Switch back to the main page frame (Python only)
- [frame_list](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/frame-list): List all frames on the page with metadata (Python only)

### Browser Automation - Agent

Run full tasks and workflows in the context of the current browser page. All methods wait for completion.

- [agent.run_task](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/agent-run-task): Run a complete AI task on the current page
- [agent.login](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/agent-login): Run a login workflow - supports Skyvern, Bitwarden, 1Password, Azure Key Vault credentials
- [agent.download_files](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/agent-download-files): Download files from the current page using AI navigation
- [agent.run_workflow](https://skyvern.com/docs/sdk-reference/browser-automation/agent-run-workflow): Run a pre-defined workflow on the current page

## Debugging & Troubleshooting

- [Using Artifacts](https://skyvern.com/docs/debugging/using-artifacts): Access recordings, screenshots, action logs, and network data for every run
- [Troubleshooting Guide](https://skyvern.com/docs/debugging/troubleshooting-guide): Diagnose common issues - step timeline analysis, prompt tuning, and when to adjust task configuration
- [Observability with Laminar](https://skyvern.com/docs/debugging/observability-with-laminar): Add tracing and observability to Skyvern automations using Laminar
- [FAQ](https://skyvern.com/docs/debugging/faq): Frequently asked questions - CAPTCHA handling, retries, artifacts, recordings, and error codes

## Self-Hosted Deployment

- [Self-Hosted Overview](https://skyvern.com/docs/self-hosted/overview): Run Skyvern on your own infrastructure with Docker or Kubernetes
- [Docker Setup](https://skyvern.com/docs/self-hosted/docker): Get Skyvern running in 10 minutes with Docker Compose
- [LLM Configuration](https://skyvern.com/docs/self-hosted/llm-configuration): Connect OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider
- [Browser Configuration](https://skyvern.com/docs/self-hosted/browser): Configure browser modes, display settings, headless vs headed, and external Chrome connections via Playwright
- [Proxy Setup](https://skyvern.com/docs/self-hosted/proxy): Configure residential proxies and geolocation for bot detection avoidance in self-hosted deployments
- [Kubernetes Deployment](https://skyvern.com/docs/self-hosted/kubernetes): Deploy Skyvern at scale with Kubernetes manifests and Helm charts
- [Storage Configuration](https://skyvern.com/docs/self-hosted/storage): Configure where Skyvern stores artifacts, recordings, and screenshots - local filesystem or S3-compatible storage

## Cookbooks

- [Cookbooks Overview](https://skyvern.com/docs/cookbooks/overview): End-to-end workflow examples for common browser automation scenarios
- [Bulk Invoice Downloader](https://skyvern.com/docs/cookbooks/bulk-invoice-downloader): Build a workflow that logs into vendor portals, finds invoices by date, and downloads them as PDFs
- [Job Application Pipeline](https://skyvern.com/docs/cookbooks/job-application-filler): Search for jobs and automatically fill out applications using stored credentials and resume data
- [Healthcare Portal Data Extraction](https://skyvern.com/docs/cookbooks/healthcare-portal-data): Extract patient demographics and billing data from OpenEMR using the Python SDK

## Cloud UI

For visual dashboard documentation - running tasks, building workflows, managing credentials, viewing results, and account settings - see the [Cloud UI Guide](https://skyvern.com/docs/cloud/overview).

## API Reference

- [OpenAPI Specification](https://skyvern.com/docs/api-reference/openapi.json): Full OpenAPI 3.x spec for all Skyvern REST API endpoints - tasks, workflows, browser sessions, browser profiles, credentials, and artifacts

## Changelog

- [Changelog](https://skyvern.com/docs/changelog): Latest features, improvements, and fixes in Skyvern
